mostFrequentlyAskedArticles.keyFocus: Qualifications, retirement age (65 years), and removal process of SC judges.
Power of the President to consult the Supreme Court and non-binding nature of the advice.

mostFrequentlyAskedArticles.topic: Comptroller and Auditor General of India
mostFrequentlyAskedArticles.keyFocus: Tenure (6 years / 65 years), Guardian of Public Purse, and submission of reports to the President.
Comparison with Article 32; enforcement of both Fundamental Rights and legal rights.
Composition (1+4 members), constitution every 5 years by the President, quasi-judicial status.
Superintendence of elections, CEC removal process, and composition.
Voting age reduction from 21 to 18 years via the 61st Constitutional Amendment Act.

mostAskedConstitutionalBodies.body: Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)
mostAskedConstitutionalBodies.commonQuestionAngles: Article 148, tenure limits, relationship with Public Accounts Committee (PAC), non-eligibility for re-appointment.
Article 280, 1st Finance Commission Chairman (K.C. Neogy), appointing authority, tenure, and quasi-judicial nature.
Article 324, 61st CAA voting age change, removal of CEC vs other ECs, scope of elections (excludes Panchayats/Municipalities).
Articles 315-323, age limits (UPSC 65 vs SPSC 62), trick question on SPSC removal (removed ONLY by President, not Governor).
Article 76, right to attend and speak in Parliament without voting rights (Article 88), pleasure of the President.

repeatedThemes.description: Matching articles and bodies with their respective retirement ages (SC/CAG/UPSC/ECI = 65 years vs HC/SPSC = 62 years).
Specifically testing exceptions like SPSC members being appointed by the Governor but removed exclusively by the President (Article 317).
Conceptual questions comparing the wider scope of High Court writ power (FRs + Legal rights) with Supreme Court writ power (FRs only).
Kesavananda Bharati (1973 - Basic Structure / 13 judges), Maneka Gandhi (1978 - Art 21 scope), and Minerva Mills (1980 - Judicial review balance).

42nd CAA (Tribunals/Mini-Constitution), 44th CAA (Right to Property shift, Art 20 & 21 non-suspension), 61st CAA (Voting age 18), 89th CAA (NCST split), 102nd CAA (NCBC status), and 104th CAA (Abolition of Anglo-Indian seats).

difficultyTrend.overallTrend: Shift from Simple Fact Retrieval to Statement-Based Analytical Questions (Tier-2 Focus)
observations: Tier-1 continues to ask direct factual questions (Article numbers, age limits, first officeholders).
Tier-2 has transitioned towards multi-statement questions (e.g., 'Which of the following statements about CAG is/are correct?').
Increased focus on distinguishing Constitutional Bodies from Statutory/Executive Bodies.
Rise in questions testing exceptions (e.g., AGI's right to speak in Parliament without voting under Article 88).

highProbabilityQuestions.expectedQuestionType: Statement-based question testing whether Governor or President removes SPSC members under Article 317.
highProbabilityQuestions.keyFactToRemember: Appointed by Governor, Removed ONLY by President.

highProbabilityQuestions.expectedQuestionType: Question on the discontinuation of Anglo-Indian nominated seats in Lok Sabha and Assemblies.
highProbabilityQuestions.keyFactToRemember: 104th CAA discontinued Anglo-Indian nomination while extending SC/ST reservations to 2030.

highProbabilityQuestions.topic: Salary vs Pension Charging Authorities for High Court Judges
highProbabilityQuestions.expectedQuestionType: Question asking which fund pays the salary vs pension of HC judges.
highProbabilityQuestions.keyFactToRemember: Salaries are charged on Consolidated Fund of the STATE; Pensions are charged on Consolidated Fund of INDIA.

highProbabilityQuestions.expectedQuestionType: Identifying the parliamentary committee that examines CAG reports.
highProbabilityQuestions.keyFactToRemember: Public Accounts Committee (PAC) acts as the friend, philosopher, and guide to the CAG.

expectedFutureAreas.area: National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC) - Article 338B
expectedFutureAreas.reasoning: Since receiving constitutional status via the 102nd CAA, questions around 338, 338A, and 338B are increasingly frequent.

expectedFutureAreas.area: Ad Hoc Judges and Acting Chief Justices (Articles 126–128)
expectedFutureAreas.reasoning: Deeper testing of judiciary articles beyond Article 124 and 143 in Tier-2 examinations.
Higher focus on 42nd Amendment additions and specialized quasi-judicial bodies.

expectedFutureAreas.area: Inter-State Water Disputes and Special Jurisdiction
expectedFutureAreas.reasoning: Exceptions to Article 131 original jurisdiction are becoming popular in statement-based questions.
